title: Integer Overflow in fromInt Function
reason: False positive. `fromInt` restricts `x` to `[-2^63, 2^63-1]`; shifting that range left by 64 bits stays within `int128` bounds, including `-2^63 << 64 = -2^127`.

title: Integer Overflow in fromUInt Function
reason: False positive. `fromUInt` restricts `x <= 2^63-1`, and `(2^63-1) << 64` still fits within the signed 64.64 fixed-point `int128` range.

title: Integer Overflow in avg Function
reason: False positive. `avg` adds two `int128` values after widening to `int256`; the sum of two `int128` values cannot overflow `int256`.

title: Integer Overflow in gavg Function
reason: False positive. `gavg` multiplies two `int128` values after widening to `int256`; that intermediate product fits within `int256`, so there is no pre-check overflow here.

title: Potential Integer Overflow in mul Function
reason: False positive. `mul` multiplies two widened `int128` values; their product fits in `int256`, so the only relevant bound is the post-shift range check already present.

title: Outdated Solidity Compiler Version
reason: Not reportable as stated. A generic complaint about using Solidity 0.7.x is not a concrete protocol vulnerability without a specific compiler bug affecting reachable code paths.
